english: stomach, to clog; sól-ba (sol ba) Cs. to purge, to cleanse; p'o-bai k'a (pho ba'i kha) Cs., the upper orifice of the stomach, joining the oesophagus; p'o-ńan (pho ngan), a weak st., bzań (bzang), a good, sound st. Cs. — p'o-t'ér (pho ther), swag-belly Sch.; p'o-nád (pho nad), disorder of the st. — 2. v. p'o (pho), above. II. pf. of ˳bó-ba ('bo ba) for p'os (phos) Glr.

also -ris (-ris) or p'o-ris (pho ris) Lt., black pepper; the col. form: p'o-ba-ril-bu (pho ba ril bu) 'stomachic pills' prob. is merely a popular etymology (similar to the English 'sparrow grass', corrupted from asparagus).
resp. for k'áń-pa (khang pa), house, dwelling; often also implying hall, castle, palace, B. and col.; slei p'o-brań (sle'i pho brang), the castle (palace) of Lé.
Schr. red paint; dúń-la p'o-ts'ós bskús-pa (dung la pho tshos bskus pa), red paint put on a shell Pth.; p'o-ts'os-ts'al (pho tshos tshal) Pth.
v. p'ó-ba-ri (pho ba ri).
raven, perh. also crow; cf. K'wá-ta (khwa ta); p'o-rog-mig (pho rog mig), medicinal herb, Wdn.
W., v. p'ó-la (pho la).
W. steel, Pers. پولاد, فولاد.
W. peppermint.
Mil. a kind of knot, complicated, and of magic virtue.
etc. v. p'a-wáń (pha wang).

W. haughtiness, pride; *p'o-so čo-če* (*pho so co ce*), to demean one's self haughtily, W.; *p'ó-so-čan* (*pho so can*), proud, haughty, puffed up: k'á-p'o (kha pho) Mil. bragging about things, which in reality one is not able to do; p'o-tsod (pho tshod), prob. the same as p'ó-so (pho so), Mil.: p'o-tsód mńón-śes ma ˳čad čig (pho tshod mngon shes ma bcad cig), do not boast of prophetic sight.
1. Wts. beam, rafter; Sch.: 'the principal beam of the roof'. — 2. v. ˳p'óg-pa ('phog pa) and ˳bóg-pa ('bog pa).

wages, pay, salary; lo- (lo-), zla- (zla-), nyin-p'ogs (nyin phogs) annual, monthly salary, daily wages; dńúl-p'ogs (dngul phogs), smár-p'ogs (smar phogs), Cs., payment in money; zóń-p'ogs (zong phogs) Cs. payment in goods. 2. providing for another person in natural produce, even without any service being done in return, e.g. the maintenance of Lamas; p'ogs-dód (phogs dod), maintenance by an allowance of money (in exceptional cases).
v. p'áń-ba (phang ba); p'óń-ba (phong ba) Glr. for p'ań-ba (phang ba).

(cf. ˳p'óńs-pa ('phongs pa)) 1. poor, needy; séms-čan nyam-t'ág-p'óńs-dgu (sems can nyam thag phongs dgu), the poor and miserable creatures, Glr.; sdúg-p'óńs-pa (sdug phongs pa), id. Stg., C. — 2. poverty.
skár-ma p'od (skar ma phod), Cs., Sch., comet.
masquerade garment with long sleeves.

1. to be able, esp. in a moral sense, to prevail on one's self, ˳bral-mi p'ód-pa ltar yód-na yań ('bral mi phod pa ltar yod na yang), although he was scarcely able to part with ... Glr.; ˳di ni mi p'ód-do ('di ni mi phod do), that I cannot do (moral impossibility) Dzl.; lta mi p'ód (lta mi phod), I cannot bear to see that, Dzl.; to be able to resist: zas źim gos bzań su-yis p'od (zas zhim gos bzang su yis phod) who can resist good food and fine clothes? hence p'ód-pa-čan (phod pa can), Cs., bold, daring; *p'od-č'uń-se* (*phod chung se*), W. timid, cowardly. — 2. to come up to, to be nearly equal in worth, with tsam(-la) (tsam(-la)): dëi bsód-nams tsam-la p'ód (de'i bsod nams tsam la phod) it is nearly of equal merit as ... Dzl.

Glr. and elsewh., p'ób-p'on (phob phon) Cs., p'ón-po(n) (phon po(n)), p'ón-to (phon to), p'od-p'ód (phod phod), W., 1. bundle, truss, of hay, straw, reeds; sheaf. C. — 2. bunch, wisp, cluster, umbel, W.; tuft, tassel; dár-p'on (dar phon), skúd-p'on (skud phon), Cs.
v. ˳bébs-pa ('bebs pa).

C., B. (W. *kó-re* (*ko re*), resp. *don-kyóg* (*don kyog*)), bowl, dish, drinking-cup, generally made of wood and carried in the bosom, to have it always ready for use; cups made of other materials are called lcágs-p'or (lcags phor), dńúl-p'or (dngul phor), gsér-p'or (gser phor), and a glass tumbler śél-p'or (shel phor). The word is also applied to vessels used for other purposes: spyín-p'or (spyin phor), glue-pot, póg-p'or (pog phor), perfuming-pan. — p'ór-p'yis (phor phyis), cloth for wiping the cup; p'or-kúg (phor kug), id.(?); p'or-śúg(śubs?) (phor shug(shubs?)), the pocket or fold in the coat for receiving the cup, C.
